2603.16574.title: Diverging Transformer Predictions for Human Sentence Processing: A Comprehensive Analysis of Agreement Attraction Effects
2603.16574.paper_summary: Transformers underlie almost all state-of-the-art language models in computational linguistics, yet their cognitive adequacy as models of human sentence processing remains disputed. In this work, we use a surprisal-based linking mechanism to systematically evaluate eleven autoregressive transformers of varying sizes and architectures on a more comprehensive set of English agreement attraction configurations than prior work. Our experiments yield mixed results: While transformer predictions generally align with human reading time data for prepositional phrase configurations, performance degrades significantly on object-extracted relative clause configurations. In the latter case, predictions also diverge markedly across models, and no model successfully replicates the asymmetric interference patterns observed in humans. We conclude that current transformer models do not explain human morphosyntactic processing, and that evaluations of transformers as cognitive models must adopt rigorous, comprehensive experimental designs to avoid spurious generalizations from isolated syntactic configurations or individual models.
2603.16574.paper_summary_zh: Transformer幾乎是計算語言學中所有最先進語言模型的基礎，但作為人類句子處理模型的認知適切性仍然存在爭議。在這項工作中，我們使用基於驚訝度的連結機制，系統性地評估了十一個不同大小和架構的自回歸Transformer，針對比以往研究更全面的英語一致性吸引配置進行評估。我們的實驗結果顯示出混合的結果：雖然Transformer的預測通常與人類在介詞短語配置上的閱讀時間數據一致，但在物件提取的關係子句配置上，性能顯著下降。在後者的情況下，預測在模型之間也顯著分歧，且沒有任何模型成功複製人類觀察到的不對稱干擾模式。我們得出結論，目前的Transformer模型無法解釋人類的形態句法處理，並且對Transformer作為認知模型的評估必須採用嚴謹、全面的實驗設計，以避免從孤立的句法配置或個別模型中得出虛假的概括。
2603.16574.author: Titus von der Malsburg et.al.
2603.16574.authors: Titus von der Malsburg, Sebastian Padó
